content.full_text:
91-5228
1853

Dick Williams: Sheriff

[Sheriff's seal]

Dec 16 2 30-PM 91

County of San Bernardino

[Sun burst seal]

December 12, 1991


Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

ATTN: John Wallace,
Counsel, Legal Division

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This is to clarify the intent of my earlier letter requesting advice on mass mailings, and to provide additional information requested by your staff.

This request for advice is on behalf of Sheriff Dick Williams.

Please find the enclosed items requested by your staff in their efforts to issue an advice letter.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Sincerely,

Paul R. Curry, Sergeant
LEGISLATIVE LIAISON

PRC/ajh


SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY SHERIFF'S DEPARTMENT
655 East Third Street • San Bernardino, California 92415-0061     Post Office Box 569 • San Bernardino, California 92402-0569

Dick Williams, Sheriff

December 2, 1991

Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

Please provide clarification regarding the Fair Political Practices Commission's permanent
Regulation 18901(b) (4), addressing newsletters produced at public expense:

QUESTION # 1

May unpaid staff receive intra-agency communications sent in the normal course of busi-
ness, which is also being sent to paid employee's? The unpaid employees use agency
equipment and vehicles and perform various law enforcement-related duties, many times
independent of salaried employees.

This is a request for a formal opinion pursuant to your informal assistance letter. Your
File: I-91-475.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,

Paul R. Curry, Sergeant
LEGISLATIVE LIAISON

PRC/ajh

SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY SHERIFF'S DEPARTMENT
655 East Third Street • San Bernardino, California 92415-0061        Post Office Box 569 • San Bernardino, California 92402-0569
